first_order_energy_correction.name: First-Order Energy Correction (Perturbation Theory)
first_order_energy_correction.description: E_n^(1) = <n|H'|n>, the first-order correction to the energy in time-independent perturbation theory.
first_order_energy_correction.search_text: First order energy correction perturbation theory expectation value perturbing Hamiltonian H prime non-degenerate Rayleigh-Schrodinger matrix element diagonal

second_order_energy_correction.name: Second-Order Energy Correction (Perturbation Theory)
second_order_energy_correction.description: E_n^(2) = sum_{m != n} |<m|H'|n>|^2 / (E_n^0 - E_m^0). Second-order correction to the energy. Sum excludes m = n.
second_order_energy_correction.search_text: Second order energy correction perturbation theory matrix elements off-diagonal transition amplitude denominator energy gap level repulsion

first_order_state_correction.name: First-Order State Correction (Perturbation Theory)
first_order_state_correction.description: |n^(1)> = sum_{m != n} <m|H'|n>/(E_n^0 - E_m^0) |m>. First-order correction to the state vector. Sum excludes m = n.
first_order_state_correction.search_text: First order state correction wavefunction perturbation theory mixing coefficient admixture superposition perturbed state vector

variational_upper_bound.name: Variational Principle (Upper Bound)
variational_upper_bound.expression: Integral(conjugate(psi_trial) * H_op * psi_trial, (x, -oo, oo)) / Integral(conjugate(psi_trial) * psi_trial, (x, -oo, oo))
variational_upper_bound.description: E_gs <= <psi_trial|H|psi_trial> / <psi_trial|psi_trial>. The expectation value of H in any trial state gives an upper bound to the ground state energy.
variational_upper_bound.search_text: Variational principle upper bound ground state energy Rayleigh-Ritz trial wavefunction minimize expectation value optimization variational method

wkb_quantization.name: WKB Quantization Condition
wkb_quantization.description: Integral of p(x) dx from x1 to x2 = (n + 1/2) pi hbar. Bohr-Sommerfeld quantization in the WKB approximation. x1, x2 are classical turning points.
wkb_quantization.search_text: WKB quantization condition semiclassical approximation Bohr Sommerfeld turning points phase integral action quantization JWKB Wentzel Kramers Brillouin

fermis_golden_rule.name: Fermi's Golden Rule
fermis_golden_rule.description: Gamma = (2*pi/hbar) |<f|H'|i>|^2 rho(E), the transition rate from initial to final state under a perturbation. rho_E is the density of final states.
fermis_golden_rule.search_text: Fermi golden rule transition rate probability per unit time matrix element density of states time-dependent perturbation theory decay rate scattering absorption emission
